techniques.evidence_quote: based on the use of long-term access keys (AKIA) and the automated activity, we surmise that the attacker was likely using leaked access keys
The attacker was using a mix of residential proxies and a VPN client, specifically the Cloudflare Warp VPN
attempting the API calls `ListSecrets` and `ListVaults` in a single AWS environment ... the attacker attempted to run each of these API calls across 17 regions in under a minute
attempts to enumerate AWS S3 buckets with the `ListBuckets` API call and then determine their contents by using the `ListObjects` API call across any available buckets
They captured additional AWS API calls indicating that the target of the campaign may have been Amazon Bedrock and the possible misuse of LLM resources

attack_chain.description: Attacker obtained and used leaked long-term AWS IAM access keys (AKIA prefix) to authenticate to victim AWS environments. The automated nature and key type strongly suggest stolen/leaked static credentials.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: based on the use of long-term access keys (AKIA) and the automated activity, we surmise that the attacker was likely using leaked access keys

attack_chain.description: Attacker routed malicious API calls through a combination of residential proxies (Vodafone UK mobile network) and the Cloudflare WARP VPN client to obscure their true origin and appear less suspicious.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The attacker was using a mix of residential proxies and a VPN client, specifically the Cloudflare Warp VPN

attack_chain.description: Attacker ran automated, multi-region enumeration of AWS Secrets Manager (ListSecrets) and S3 Glacier (ListVaults) across 17 AWS regions in under a minute to discover available secrets and vault resources.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attempting the API calls `ListSecrets` and `ListVaults` in a single AWS environment ... the attacker attempted to run each of these API calls across 17 regions in under a minute

attack_chain.description: Attacker enumerated S3 buckets via ListBuckets and then inventoried contents of accessible buckets via ListObjects, successfully listing objects in several buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: attempts to enumerate AWS S3 buckets with the `ListBuckets` API call and then determine their contents by using the `ListObjects` API call across any available buckets

attack_chain.description: Corroborated by Lacework research, the ultimate goal of the campaign was identified as targeting Amazon Bedrock LLM resources for unauthorized use (LLMJacking), abusing the victim's cloud AI/ML quota and incurring costs.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: They captured additional AWS API calls indicating that the target of the campaign may have been Amazon Bedrock and the possible misuse of LLM resources

initial_access_vector: Stolen or leaked long-term AWS IAM access keys (AKIA) used to authenticate directly to victim AWS environments via automated tooling
